source_paper_title: Lipschitz Stability of Least-Squares Problems Regularized by Functions with C2-Cone Reducible Conjugates
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Cui_2026, title={Lipschitz Stability of Least-Squares Problems Regularized by Functions with C2-Cone Reducible Conjugates}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2024.0692}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2024.0692}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Cui, Ying and Hoheisel, Tim and Nghia, Tran T. context:
Page 15, end of Section 3 (discussion immediately after Theorem 3.8).

By fixing the parameter \(b=\bar b\), our condition (45) is also sufficient for the Lipschitz stability of solution mappings \(S(\cdot,\bar b,\bar\mu)\), \(S(\cdot,\bar b,\cdot)\), and \(S(\bar A,\bar b,\cdot)\) around the corresponding points due to the implication \([(i)\Rightarrow(ii)]\) in Theorem 3.8. Whether it is necessary is an open question.
title: Characterize when the Lipschitz-stability condition is necessary under fixed b setting
background_latex:
Let \(X,Y\) be Euclidean spaces, \(A\in \mathcal L(X,Y)\) a linear operator, \(b\in Y\) a data vector, and \(\mu>0\) a scalar. Let \(g:X\to \mathbb R\cup\{+\infty\}\) be closed, proper, and convex, and denote its Fenchel conjugate by \(g^*:X\to \mathbb R\cup\{+\infty\}\).

Consider the regularized least-squares problem
\[
\min_{x\in X}\ \frac{1}{2\mu}\|Ax-b\|^2+g(x),
\]
with solution mapping
\[
S(A,b,\mu):=\operatorname{argmin}_{x\in X}\Big\{\frac{1}{2\mu}\|Ax-b\|^2+g(x)\Big\}.
\]
Fix a triple \((\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\) with \(\bar\mu>0\), and let \(\bar x\in S(\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\). Define
\[
\bar z:=\frac{1}{\bar\mu}\,\bar A^*(\bar b-\bar A\bar x)\in X.
\]
For a convex set \(\Omega\subseteq X\), its parallel subspace is \(\operatorname{par}\Omega:=\operatorname{span}(\Omega-x)\) for any \(x\in\Omega\). For a convex function \(h\), \(\partial h(u)\) denotes its (convex-analysis) subdifferential at \(u\).

The paper studies the condition
\[
\ker \bar A\ \cap\ \operatorname{par}\big(\partial g^*(\bar z)\big)=\{0\},
\]
which is known (under additional hypotheses in the paper) to be equivalent to local single-valuedness and local Lipschitz continuity of \((A,b,\mu)\mapsto S(A,b,\mu)\) around \((\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\).

One may also consider restricted-parameter solution mappings where one parameter is held fixed, e.g.
\[
(A,\mu)\mapsto S(A,\bar b,\mu),\qquad A\mapsto S(A,\bar b,\bar\mu),\qquad \mu\mapsto S(\bar A,\bar b,\mu).
\]
The question is whether the same first-order condition above is not only sufficient but also necessary for Lipschitz stability of these restricted solution mappings.
problem_definition_latex:
Assume \(g^*\) is \(C^2\)-cone reducible at \(\bar z:=\frac{1}{\bar\mu}\,\bar A^*(\bar b-\bar A\bar x)\) for some \(\bar x\in S(\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\).

Determine whether the condition
\[
\ker \bar A\ \cap\ \operatorname{par}\big(\partial g^*(\bar z)\big)=\{0\}
\]
is necessary (not just sufficient) for local Lipschitz continuity of the solution mapping when \(b\) is fixed, e.g. for one or more of the restricted mappings
\[
(A,\mu)\mapsto S(A,\bar b,\mu),\qquad A\mapsto S(A,\bar b,\bar\mu),\qquad \mu\mapsto S(\bar A,\bar b,\mu),
\]
around \((\bar A,\bar\mu)\), \(\bar A\), or \(\bar\mu\), respectively.

location_in_paper: Page 15, end of Section 3 (discussion immediately after Theorem 3.8).
open_problem_quote: By fixing the parameter \(b=\bar b\), our condition (45) is also sufficient for the Lipschitz stability of solution mappings \(S(\cdot,\bar b,\bar\mu)\), \(S(\cdot,\bar b,\cdot)\), and \(S(\bar A,\bar b,\cdot)\) around the corresponding points due to the implication \([(i)\Rightarrow(ii)]\) in Theorem 3.8. subject_classification: variational analysis and stability of convex optimization solution mappings
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can attempt to search for low-dimensional examples \((X,Y,g,\bar A,\bar b,\bar\mu)\) where the restricted mapping is Lipschitz yet \(\ker\bar A\cap \operatorname{par}(\partial g^*(\bar z))\neq\{0\}\), but verifying local Lipschitz stability rigorously may require nontrivial analytic checks beyond brute-force enumeration.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Automated evaluation of candidate constructions would require reliably certifying local Lipschitz continuity of a set-valued argmin mapping under parameter perturbations, which is not straightforward to turn into a robust computational fitness function without substantial additional theory or symbolic/numerical certification tools.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
